good_side: A small game can prove a mechanic quickly and make the idea easy to share.
bad_side: Games sprawl through art, levels, effects, menus, and tuning before the core mechanic is fun.
core_proof: A player can understand and replay the core mechanic within one minute.
core_workflow: Start a round, play the main mechanic, receive feedback, and restart quickly.
workflow_steps: Use the main input to affect game state
entity_purpose: Tracks the current round and player progress
relationships: Updated by input and simulation systems
risk: The game has presentation but the mechanic is not fun or legible.
mitigation: Build a graybox mechanic and replay loop before final assets.
open_question: What is the one player action that should feel good in v1?
later_task: Add content, polish, and juice after the mechanic works.
core_assertion: The round loop can start, resolve, and restart without broken state.
e2e_check: Manually play one round on desktop and mobile if web-based
acceptance: Round loop is playable and restartable

stack.FRONTEND: Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or existing game framework
stack.STORAGE: Local state only unless scores are core
stack.DEPLOYMENT_TARGET: Static host or existing app host
